reasoning: The women's 100m breaststroke long course world record (1:04.13) was set by Lilly King in 2017 and has not been closely approached recently. The fastest active swimmers (Tang Qianting, Ruta Meilutyte) have best times roughly half a second slower (1:04.6-ish), and the top Americans at the 2025 US Trials swam no faster than 1:06. Most nations have already held their qualifying trials for the major upcoming event, the 2025 World Aquatics Championships. Since the swimming portion of the World Championships takes place from July 27 to August 3, 2025, there are virtually no opportunities for the record to be broken before the first two resolution dates (June 29 and July 22), as elite swimmers will be tapering. Therefore, the probability is extremely high for the first two dates. The primary threat to the record is the World Championships themselves. I assign roughly a 6-7% chance that Tang or another competitor manages a massive drop to break the record there. Afterwards, the rest of the year typically focuses on short-course (25m) events rather than long-course, making another subsequent long-course record break very unlikely.
